sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the diff more carefully:
BEFORE SPACE:
- W: [[40, 32], [40, 35]] - white bar 4 pixels wide at row 40, cols 32-35 (just above green)
AFTER SPACE:
- W: [[36, 32], [36, 35], [40, 35], [40, 32]] - white bar now has 16 pixels, boundary shows it expanded to form a rectangle from rows 36-40, cols 32-35
So SPACE didn't transform the white bar into a blue token. Instead, the white bar expanded from a 1x4 horizontal bar to a 5x4 rectangle (rows 36-40, cols 32-35). This rectangle now contains the bottom-center target's blue square at [37, 33]-[38, 34].
Wait, looking more carefully at the boundary: [[36, 32], [36, 35], [40, 35], [40, 32]] - this is a 4x5 rectangle (rows 36-40, cols 32-35), with 16 pixels. The blue square at [37, 33]-[38, 34] is inside this rectangle.
So SPACE seems to expand the white indicator bar into a larger region. Maybe it's creating a "selection box" or "collection zone". Let me check if the blue square inside gets absorbed or if something else happens.
The blue square at [37, 33]-[38, 34] is still there after SPACE. So the white rectangle now surrounds it. Maybe I need to move the green player to overlap with the blue target to "collect" it.
Let me try moving UP to overlap with the blue square at rows 37-38.

# Green at rows 41-43, cols 32-35

action(['UP'])
